results.possibleIdentity: Dr. Edward B. Goldman was a prominent research scientist and associate professor at the University of Rochester during the late 1960s and 1970s. He was a member of the Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Sciences and played a significant role in the university's Laser Fusion Feasibility Project (now the Laboratory for Laser Energetics). His technical expertise spanned mechanical engineering, aeronautics, aerospace sciences, and ultrasonics, with a particular focus on the hydrodynamic modeling of gases and plasmas. Goldman authored several influential papers in the Journal of the Acoustical Society of America regarding the absorption and dispersion of ultrasonic waves in gas mixtures. In the early 1970s, he focused on the physics of laser-driven fusion, developing numerical Lagrangian codes to simulate the dynamics of laser-imploded pellets and investigate thermonuclear neutron yields. His work often involved advanced mathematics and computer programming to perform numerical experiments. He is also noted for his potential association with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that fostered collaborations between the technical community and artists during that era.
